food_subgroup: Beef burger with bun and condiments

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002383 (hamburger) is the closest parent class. No specific code exists for a double-patty fast-food beef burger from Jollibee.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP (Hamburger) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for a double-patty variant or Jollibee-branded product.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226789007 (Hamburger) is the closest parent concept. No specific code for double-patty fast-food burger.

serving.natural_language_portion: One Classic Jolliburger Double Patty burger, approximately 220 g, consisting of two beef patties, a toasted bun, lettuce, tomato, and condiments.

nutrient_source.title: Hamburger, double patty with condiments and vegetables — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 21110)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for a fast-food hamburger with double patty, condiments, and vegetables (NDB 21110 / FDC ID 173967). The Jollibee Classic Jolliburger Double Patty is a comparable double-beef-patty burger with bun, lettuce, tomato, and condiments. Jollibee Singapore does not publish full nutrition panels publicly; USDA SR Legacy double-patty hamburger is the closest approved analogue. Values are per 100 g of edible portion.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Jollibee Classic Jolliburger Double Patty. GI of 66 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for a white-bread-bun hamburger (GI ~66, medium category), consistent with Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (66 × 48.4) / 100 ≈ 32.0, using serving size of 220 g and carbohydrate_g of 22.0 per 100 g (22.0 × 2.2 = 48.4 g per serving).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g; ~1056 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this burger as a single serving provides approximately 44% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg, and regular consumption of high-sodium fast food is associated with elevated blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium-to-high glycemic load (~32 per serving) and refined carbohydrate from white bun
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this burger only occasionally and in reduced portions, as the high glycemic load from the refined white bun combined with the high caloric density can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~4.8 g per 100 g), trans fat (~0.5 g per 100 g), and high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this burger due to its saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium content, all of which are associated with increased LDL cholesterol and cardiovascular risk when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Classic Jolliburger Double Patty is a fast-food beef burger sold by Jollibee, a Filipino quick-service restaurant chain with outlets in Singapore including Bugis Village. It consists of two seasoned and grilled beef patties stacked in a toasted white-flour bun, accompanied by fresh lettuce, tomato slices, pickles, mayonnaise, and ketchup. The burger is certified Halal in Singapore. The double-patty format delivers a hearty, savoury flavour profile with a soft bun, juicy beef, and a mild sweetness from the condiments. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense at approximately 265 kcal per 100 g, with meaningful protein (~14.5 g per 100 g) from the beef patties, moderate fat (~13.5 g per 100 g) including saturated and trans fats, and refined carbohydrates (~22 g per 100 g) primarily from the bun. Sodium content is high (~480 mg per 100 g), making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The glycemic load per serving is high (~32), posing a risk for blood glucose spikes in people with Type 2 Diabetes. The saturated fat and trans fat content are relevant considerations for cardiovascular health. This item is best consumed occasionally as part of a balanced diet and is not recommended for daily consumption by individuals with cardiometabolic conditions.
common_user_queries: What is the calorie count of Jollibee double burger?
Is the Jolliburger halal in Singapore?
How much protein in Jollibee double patty burger?
Is Jollibee burger suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: Jollibee Singapore does not publish a full nutrition panel for the Classic Jolliburger Double Patty on an approved primary source. Nutrient values are adapted from the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for a comparable fast-food double-patty hamburger with condiments and vegetables. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for a white-bun hamburger. Confidence is medium because the match is compositionally defensible but not an exact brand-specific measurement.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food category. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food category. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food category. All other -1 values reflect nutrients not reported by the cited source. No core macro is zero; all four core macros are positive and nutritionally plausible for a double-patty beef burger. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for a white-bun hamburger; no direct GI measurement for the Jollibee Jolliburger exists in approved sources. Serving size of 220 g is estimated based on typical double-patty fast-food burger weight; actual product weight may vary. Halal status is based on Jollibee Singapore's publicly stated Halal certification. Trans fat value of 0.5 g per 100 g is derived from the USDA SR Legacy analogue and may differ from the actual product formulation.

improvement_actions_taken: Populated all mandatory multilingual local_names (Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) for Fast Food / Singapore context.
Adapted nutrient values from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 173967 (double-patty hamburger with condiments and vegetables) as the closest approved analogue.
Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) with explicit source-not-reported justifications in notes.
Estimated GI of 66 from University of Sydney GI Database analogous white-bun hamburger entry; documented estimation basis in gi_source.
Calculated glycemic_load_per_serving as 32.0 using serving size 220 g and carbohydrate_g 22.0 per 100 g.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases list are perfectly consistent — all strings in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in aliases.
Set ontology match_type values to 'closest_parent' for all three ontology systems with honest gap notes.
Included Type 2 Diabetes with human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' and machine key 'type2_diabetes'.
